prompt.content:
Evaluate whether Meridian Consulting Group (UK B2B consultancy, 150 staff) should SWITCH to a competitor SaaS tool, STAY with the current tool, or issue a HOLD verdict.

Compliance has already been checked — the competitor passed all hard blocks.

SWITCH — competitor resolves one or more key push issues AND (ROI threshold met OR clear operational gains such as unblocked revenue or removed compliance risk).
STAY   — competitor does not resolve the key push issues, push signals are weak, or the ROI and operational case is insufficient.
HOLD   — switch case is real but a named condition must be met first: feature in beta (not GA), contract timing, or insufficient proof of delivery.

Signal position matters: pre-GA language (beta, preview, roadmap) in competitor changes = competitor nearly ready → HOLD (wait for GA). Pre-GA language only in buried notes = competitor not viable → STAY (not HOLD). Trust the Hold signal field: if NONE, there is no hold condition regardless of what notes contain.

If a Disqualifier field is present: the competitor has an unresolved delivery gap — verdict is STAY (not HOLD). A disqualifier is not a hold condition.

ROI threshold: £1,200/yr net. Staff rate: £48/hr. Migration: max 15 hours.
A feature improvement that resolves a HIGH push issue can justify SWITCH even if direct ROI is negative, provided the operational gain is clearly articulated.

Both the current tool AND the competitor can improve simultaneously — evaluate the delta: which improvements matter more for this business right now?

Format your response exactly as:
PUSH SIGNALS:
  - <current tool issue> — Severity: HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW
  [or: None flagged this period]

PULL SIGNALS:
  - <competitor change> — Substance: CONCRETE/VAGUE
  [or: None identified]

COMPLIANCE: PASSED
ROI: Migration £<X> | Annual net £<Y> → Threshold MET/NOT MET

HOLD CONDITION: NONE
[or: <reason if a named condition blocks switching>]

ANALYSIS: <2-4 sentences — name the key push issue(s), state whether competitor changes resolve them, weigh ROI or operational gain, and state hold condition if any>
VERDICT: <SWITCH|STAY|HOLD>

Category: analytics — current tool: InsightDeck
Must-have: real-time connectors (NexusCRM/VaultLedger/TaskBridge), self-serve embedded reporting, data lineage/audit trail, mobile dashboards.

Current tool known issues:
- NexusCRM sync hits API rate limits above 50k records — dashboards stale by up to 4 hours
- No self-serve embedded reporting for clients — requires export and manual PDF
- ML-based anomaly detection promised on roadmap since Q2 2023, still not shipped
- No mobile view — dashboards non-functional on phones/tablets
- Data lineage tracking absent — compliance team flagging this for upcoming ISO audit

Changes this period:
  Current tool: - Data lineage tracking still absent — compliance team has escalated to ISO audit team
- No embedded client reporting — manual PDF export still required for every client review
  Competitor: - New funnel analysis views and cohort segmentation for product teams
- A/B testing module updated with multi-variant support
- Real-time event tracking latency reduced to sub-100ms

Buried signals / notes:
- PulseMetrics is designed for product and user behaviour analytics (Mixpanel/Amplitude class tool) — not business intelligence
- No white-label embedded reporting — hard requirement for client delivery use case
- No data lineage tracking — this is the primary compliance gap flagged for ISO audit
- UK data residency not available
- Competitor changes are product analytics features with no relevance to Meridian's BI requirements
- Poor fit for consulting and financial reporting use case

ROI: Migration: £720, Annual net: £120, Threshold: NOT MET
Compliance: PASSED
Hold signal: NONE

chosen:
PUSH SIGNALS:
  - Data lineage tracking still absent — compliance team has escalated to ISO audit team — Severity: HIGH
  - No embedded client reporting — manual PDF export still required for every client review — Severity: HIGH
  - NexusCRM sync hits API rate limits above 50k records — dashboards stale by up to 4 hours — Severity: LOW (ongoing)
  - No self-serve embedded reporting for clients — requires export and manual PDF — Severity: HIGH (ongoing)

PULL SIGNALS:
  - New funnel analysis views and cohort segmentation for product teams — Substance: VAGUE
  - A/B testing module updated with multi-variant support — Substance: VAGUE
  - Real-time event tracking latency reduced to sub-100ms — Substance: CONCRETE

COMPLIANCE: PASSED
ROI: Migration £720 | Annual net £+120 → Threshold NOT MET

HOLD CONDITION: NONE

ANALYSIS: ROI threshold not met at £+120/yr net. The competitor changes do not justify migration costs. Hold signal: NONE. STAY.
VERDICT: STAY
rejected:
PUSH SIGNALS:
  - Data lineage tracking still absent — compliance team has escalated to ISO audit team — Severity: HIGH
  - No embedded client reporting — manual PDF export still required for every client review — Severity: HIGH
  - NexusCRM sync hits API rate limits above 50k records — dashboards stale by up to 4 hours — Severity: LOW (ongoing)
  - No self-serve embedded reporting for clients — requires export and manual PDF — Severity: HIGH (ongoing)

PULL SIGNALS:
  - New funnel analysis views and cohort segmentation for product teams — Substance: VAGUE
  - A/B testing module updated with multi-variant support — Substance: VAGUE
  - Real-time event tracking latency reduced to sub-100ms — Substance: CONCRETE

COMPLIANCE: PASSED
ROI: Migration £720 | Annual net £+120 → Threshold NOT MET

HOLD CONDITION: NONE

ANALYSIS: The switch case has merit — PulseMetrics offers improvements. However, a hold condition is present: NONE. Until this is resolved, the switch should wait. Compliance PASSED. ROI threshold not met at £+120/yr net. HOLD.
VERDICT: HOLD

Category: finance — current tool: VaultLedger
Must-have: multi-currency invoicing (EUR native), real-time Barclays feed, IFRS 15 compliance, audit trail PDF with digital signature, 24/7 SLA.

Current tool known issues:
- No native multi-currency — EUR invoices require manual conversion and journal entries
- Reconciliation on datasets >5,000 rows takes 8–12 minutes; causes month-end bottleneck
- Revenue recognition module not IFRS 15 compliant — workaround in use
- No real-time bank feed for Barclays Business accounts (only overnight sync)
- Audit trail export is CSV only — external auditors have requested PDF with digital signature

Changes this period:
  Current tool: - EUR invoicing still manual — two payment delays in last 12 months due to missing native multi-currency
- IFRS 15 workaround still in use — external audit flagged as a compliance risk
  Competitor: - Multi-currency invoicing (35+ currencies including EUR, USD, CHF) now live on Business tier
- Real-time bank feeds for Barclays, HSBC, Lloyds, NatWest, Monzo, Starling now available
- Fast reconciliation engine: 200k rows processed in under 90 seconds
- Audit trail export: PDF with digital signature now available on all tiers
- UK payroll sync with PayAxis now native (no middleware)

Buried signals / notes:
- IFRS 15 revenue recognition module planned Q3 2025 — not yet GA; workaround still required
- IFRS 15 is a must-have finance feature for Meridian — non-compliance is an active audit risk
- All other critical pain points are resolved by ExactSpend, but IFRS 15 gap remains
- Correct verdict is HOLD: reassess once IFRS 15 module confirmed GA
- Do not issue SWITCH while a must-have feature is still on roadmap and not delivered

ROI: Migration: £720, Annual net: £420, Threshold: NOT MET
Compliance: PASSED
Hold signal: Correct verdict is HOLD: reassess once IFRS 15 module confirmed GA

  - IFRS 15 workaround still in use — external audit flagged as a compliance risk — Severity: HIGH
  - No native multi-currency — EUR invoices require manual conversion and journal entries — Severity: HIGH (ongoing)
  - Reconciliation on datasets >5,000 rows takes 8–12 minutes; causes month-end bottleneck — Severity: LOW (ongoing)

- Performance review module is outdated — no continuous feedback, only annual cycles

- GDPR right-to-erasure requests must be handled manually via support ticket

Category: project_mgmt — current tool: TaskBridge
Nice-to-have: built-in time tracking, client portal with approvals, auto-updating Gantt charts, resource capacity planning.

- No time-tracking built in — staff use a separate tool (ChronoLog) at £95/mo

- No resource capacity planning — managers track utilisation in spreadsheets
- Slack integration was deprecated in TaskBridge v4.1; now requires Zapier workaround

- High inactive seat rate signals tool-fit mismatch — staff not adopting TaskBridge at scale

Signal position matters: pre-GA language (beta, preview, roadmap) in competitor changes = competitor nearly ready → HOLD (wait for GA). Pre-GA language only in buried notes = competitor not viable → STAY (not HOLD). Trust the Hold signal field: if NONE, there is no hold condition regardless of what notes contain.

If a Disqualifier field is present: the competitor has an unresolved delivery gap — verdict is STAY (not HOLD). A disqualifier is not a hold condition.

ROI threshold: £1,200/yr net. Staff rate: £48/hr. Migration: max 15 hours.
A feature improvement that resolves a HIGH push issue can justify SWITCH even if direct ROI is negative, provided the operational gain is clearly articulated.

Both the current tool AND the competitor can improve simultaneously — evaluate the delta: which improvements matter more for this business right now?

- LedgerFlow directly resolves the three HIGH push issues flagged in prior reviews

Category: analytics — current tool: InsightDeck

- ML-based anomaly detection promised on roadmap since Q2 2023, still not shipped
- No mobile view — dashboards non-functional on phones/tablets
- Data lineage tracking absent — compliance team flagging this for upcoming ISO audit

  Competitor: - New funnel analysis views and cohort segmentation for product teams
- A/B testing module updated with multi-variant support

- No data lineage tracking — this is the primary compliance gap flagged for ISO audit
- UK data residency not available
